task_completion.description: Verify that the @Entity annotation has been added to the Category class.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In a screenshot showing Category.java, look for the '@Entity' annotation (imported from jakarta.persistence) placed directly above the 'public class Category' declaration.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Product entity fields are correctly mapped to the database columns.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In a screenshot showing Product.java, look for '@Column(name="product_id")' above the 'id' field, '@Column(name="sku_code")' above the 'sku' field, and '@Column(name="unit_price")' above the 'price' field.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Product entity has a ManyToOne relationship with the Category entity.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In a screenshot showing Product.java, look for a field of type 'Category' (e.g., 'private Category category;') replacing the old 'categoryId' field. It must be annotated with '@ManyToOne' and '@JoinColumn(name="category_id")'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the integration tests pass successfully.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In a terminal screenshot, look for the output of 'mvn test' or 'mvn clean test' showing 'BUILD SUCCESS' and indicating that 'MappingIntegrationTest' (or all tests) passed without errors.

integrity.description: Ensure the schema.sql file was not modified to bypass fixing the Java entities.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that there are no edits to src/main/resources/schema.sql. The agent should only edit the Java files.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used a text editor or IDE to modify the Java files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing Category.java and Product.java open in an editor like VS Code, IntelliJ, nano, or vim, rather than just using 'echo' commands to overwrite files.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent genuinely ran the Maven tests.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for standard Maven test output in the terminal, including the Spring Boot startup banner and test execution logs, rather than a mocked or printed success message.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project is a Spring Boot 3.1.5 application using Java 17 and Jakarta Persistence API (jakarta.persistence.*). The goal is to fix entity mappings to match an existing H2 database schema. Category.java requires the @Entity annotation. Product.java requires specific column mappings: @Column(name="product_id") for id, @Column(name="sku_code") for sku, and @Column(name="unit_price") for price. Additionally, Product.java must replace its 'Long categoryId' field with a 'Category category' field annotated with @ManyToOne and @JoinColumn(name="category_id"). The schema.sql file must NOT be modified. Success is verified by running 'mvn test' and seeing MappingIntegrationTest pass.
